rulings.Case Title: THE BEST SERVICE CO., A PARTNERSHIP VS. CHANH CU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend Renewal Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THE BEST SERVICE CO.'s motion To Amend Renewal Of Judgment. Denied without prejudice. This matter was originally set for hearing on January 16, 2013. Best Service Co. failed to provide courtesy copies as required by Local Rule 2.6B. The Court issued an order on January 16, 2013 continuing this matter until February 1, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. to give Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The Court order stated in part that "Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of his moving papers to Department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than January 18, 2013 at 4p.m., or the motion will be denied." Plaintiff has failed to provide courtesy copies of the papers in compliance with Local Rule 2.6B and the Court's January 16, 2013 order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Responses Or To Deem Certain Requests For Admission (Set One) Admitted And For Ruther Responses To Certain Form Interrogatories (Set Two) Against Pltf C. Overaa & Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANTS U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC., STEVEN CASE, RICHARD NORRIS, JOHN BRIMMER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Further Responses Or To Deem Certain Requests For Admission (Set One) Admitted And For Further Responses To Certain Form Interrogatories (Set Two) Against Plaintiff C. Overaa & Co., Inc. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ANGEL BUESO VS. PETER C. FRIEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANTs PETER FRIEL & FM CONSTRUCTION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Denied. The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." CCP §438(d). The Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice of its Exhibits A, B, D, E, and F. The Court denies Defendants' request for judicial notice of Exhibit C, a letter apparently from a private entity. The Complaint is properly pled and Defendant has failed to show in this motion that Plaintiff's claim is barred. See Labor Code § 3715(a); Felix v. Workmen's Comp. Appeals Board, 41 Cal App. 3d 759, 765 (1974). Ruling on Defendants' objections to evidence (by objection numbers): Declaration of Juan Simon: Overrule: 1, 4 (exhibit itself is admissible and is the basis on which the motion is made) Sustain: 2, 3, 5 (irrelevant), 6, 7, 8, 9. Declaration of Angel Bueso in Support of Opposition to Motion by Defendants for Judgment on the Pleadings: Sustain: 10 (relevance), 11. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ZACKS & FREEDMAN, P.C. VS. DAMIEN QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF ZACKS & FREEDMAN, P.C.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant has raised a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Martina Quinn was responsible for paying Plaintiff's legal fees and costs. (See Martina Quinn Decl. 7-8, 12-13, 15-16.) The Martina Quinn declaration and the statements made therein are not barred by the parol evidence rule because the "Engagement Letter" (Zacks Decl., Ex. B, p.1) is ambiguous on its face. The first paragraph of Ex. B. states in pertinent part, "This engagement letter sets forth the terms of our firm's performance of legal services on behalf of Martina Quinn ("Client"). Damien Quinn ("Payor") has agreed to pay for our fees and costs." The meaning argued by Defendant is one to which the written contract terms are reasonably susceptible and extrinsic evidence is thus admissible to explain the ambiguity. (See Code of Civ. Pro 1856(g); Singh v. Southland Stone, U.S.A., Inc. (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 338, 352-53; Casa Herrera, Inc. v. Beydoun (2004) 32 Cal.4th 336, 343; Pacific Gas & E. Co. v. G.W. Thomas Drayage etc. Co. (1968) 69 Cal.2d 33, 39-40.) As such, there exists a triable issue of material fact as to Plaintiff's asserted Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 18, 21, 22; First Cause of Action UMF 77, 80, 81; Second Cause of Action UMF 136, 139, 140; Third Cause of Action: 195, 198, 199; Fourth Cause of Action: 274, 277, 278. In addition, Defendant has raised disputed additional issues of material fact: 320-323; 331-334; 342-345; 353-356. Ruling on Plaintiff's Objections to Declaration of Martha Quinn (by objection number): Sustained: 1; the remainder of Plaintiff's objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: TOBI.COM, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CATHERINE CHOW, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANTs CATHERINE CHOW, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT, CORINA NURIMBA, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT, WILSON CHOW, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT, DAVID LIU, A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT & AZALEA BOUTIQUE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's Motion For Relief From Default. Granted. Defendants have demonstrated that they are entitled to mandatory relief. (CCP § 473.) Although attorneys Woo and Cohen attempt to "deflect fault" from themselves, their declarations have the "opposite effect" and meet the requirements for mandatory relief under CCP § 473(b). Carmel Ltd. v. Tavoussi (2009) 175 Cal. App. 4th 393, 401. There is no evidence of neglect or wrongdoing by defense counsels' clients. Defendants shall pay Plaintiff's reasonable attorney fees in seeking default, in the amount of $4750. CCP 473(c)(1)(C). As a result of defendants' counsel's inaction, Plaintiff filed the request for default and took necessary steps to prepare for a prove-up hearing and have a default judgment entered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY KRAMER VS. KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK & GONZO LLP, A LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And/Or To Quash Defts' A) Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Shelly Kramer And Request For Documents, B) Form Interrogatories And C) Document Demands
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 1, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 7, Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And/Or To Quash Defendants' A) Notice Of Taking Deposition Of Shelly Kramer And Request For Documents, B) Form Interrogatories And C) Document Demands Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No tentative; parties to appear. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: URS CORPORATION DBA URS CORPORATION AMERICAS, A VS. UNIVERSAL MOLDING EXTRUSION COMPANY, INC. DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Universal Molding Extrusion Company, Inc. Dba Universal Mold And Extrusion'S Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 19, Defendant Universal Molding Extrusion Company, Inc. Dba Universal Mold And Extrusion'S Motion To Transfer Venue. Denied. Defendant has not met its burden to show that venue is improper in San Francisco. Plaintiff's declarations are not inconsistent with the allegations in the Complaint. The Complaint alleges that performance of the contract at issue occurred, in part, in San Francisco. See Complaint, 5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, February 1, 2013, Line 17, Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Motion to continue by Defendant is granted. This matter is continued to February 21, 2013 in order to permit Mr. Vasquez to file a substantive opposition addressing the merits to the Order to Show Cause. Any opposition shall be filed and served no later than February 8, 2013 at 4 pm. City and County of San Francisco may file a reply to this opposition. Any reply shall be filed and served no later than February 13, 2013 at 4 pm. Service of the opposition and reply shall be effectuated by personal delivery, fax or email. Courtesy copies of the opposition and reply shall be provided to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, on the date of filing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
